mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflect underlying labor demand and income growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135046K at snapshot, up from 134832K previous month
failure_condition: If 6 consecutive months of positive PAYEMS are followed by negative SPX 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 7.5% signals that the labor market is healing faster than expected, prompting upward revisions to growth expectations and immediate positive equity market reaction within 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.7% at snapshot, previous month 7.9%
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 7.5% and SPX 5-day return is negative in more than 50% of occurrences over a 3-year test window, or average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) provide a strong signal that consumer demand is broadening from housing into general consumption, indicating sustainable economic expansion that lifts equity valuations over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 890K, RSAFS at 421399M at snapshot
failure_condition: If months with both HOUST and RSAFS increasing show a negative average SPX 20-day return, or if the joint signal does not outperform a single-series signal in terms of rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is trapped at the zero lower bound (<0.5%), a rise in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals that demand is accelerating faster than supply, raising fears of premature policy tightening and causing negative forward equity returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound regime with nascent inflation pressure
failure_condition: If in periods where FEDFUNDS<0.5% and CPIAUCSL MoM>0.3%, SPX 20-day returns are positive more often than negative, or average return > 0.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) provides the broadest measure of economic output growth; positive trend growth in GDP is fundamental support for corporate earnings and thus the stock market over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 grows at >=2% annualized and SPX 60-day returns are negative over the subsequent quarter in more than 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) crosses above 100, it signals that manufacturing has recovered to pre-recession levels, suggesting broad-based supply-side health that supports equity market gains over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after INDPRO crosses above 100, the average SPX 20-day return is negative or not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and increase in payroll employment provides a robust corroborated signal of strengthening labor market, reinforcing income and spending growth and driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from 7.9% to 7.7% MoM, PAYEMS rose from 134832K to 135046K
failure_condition: If in months with both UNRATE decline and PAYEMS increase, the SPX 20-day return is not consistently positive (accuracy < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: In a housing recovery regime where starts are below 1,000K but rising month-over-month, continued increases in HOUST signal sustained recovery momentum that leads to positive equity returns over 5 trading days as payrolls and construction spending lift sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 890K, up from 880K previous month
regime_hint: housing recovery regime (HOUST < 1000 and MoM increase > 2%)
failure_condition: If in months when HOUST < 1000 and MoM rise >2%, the average SPX 5-day return is zero or negative.
